summary: The task maps directly to oauth-consent-and-token-runtime.md and is complete in substance. The implementation provides issued-token list/detail/filter UI, reconstructs claim and endpoint permission metadata without returning stored raw token values, persists revocation via OAuthIssuedToken.revokedAt, and shares revocation enforcement through the existing Bearer validator used by OAuth REST/MCP runtime paths. Required deterministic gates are reported passing, including unit tests, the @token-revocation E2E, and full verify.
satisfied_exit_criteria: Token UI exposes claims and endpoint_permissions metadata without raw access-token redisplay.
Revocation is persisted on issued-token records and subsequent Bearer validation rejects revoked tokens with 401 behavior.
Filters distinguish active, expired, and revoked tokens; unit coverage includes expired and E2E covers active/revoked behavior through the UI.
npm run test:e2e -- --grep @token-revocation passed in the deterministic check summary.
All required checks passed: npm run test:unit, npm run test:e2e -- --grep @token-revocation, and npm run verify.
Scope stayed within issued-token UI/API/runtime revocation hardening and did not broaden into unrelated OAuth grants or discovery work.
